video:video.video:title: Republicans launch a voting bill debate that could last days or even weeks
video:video.video:description: Republicans launched an unprecedented effort on Tuesday to hold the Senate floor and talk for days about a bill that they know won't pass — an attempt to capture public attention on legislation requiring stricter voter registration rules.

video:video.video:title: Suspected meteor falling over Cleveland could be seen from far away
video:video.video:description: A suspected meteor that fell from the sky over the Cleveland area shook homes and startled residents who heard a boom that some compared to an explosion.

video:video.video:title: One dead in Ukrainian drone attack on Russia’s Krasnodar region
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian drone hit an apartment building in the southern Russian city of Krasnodar, killing one resident, according to the city’s head Yevgeny Naumov.

video:video.video:title: Kenya experiments with giving government workers menstrual leave
video:video.video:description: It started with a casual lunch conversation between a county governor and his cabinet ministers about a colleague’s menstrual pain.

video:video.video:title: Fuel costs rise as Iran war continues to disrupt flow of oil
video:video.video:description: Drivers in the U.S. are now facing the highest prices they've seen at the pump in nearly two and a half years. (AP Video: Mingson Lau)

video:video.video:title: What is meningitis B and why is there an outbreak in Kent, UK?
video:video.video:description: Two young people have died: a school pupil in a nearby town and a student at the University of Kent, in Canterbury - 60 miles south-east of London.

video:video.video:title: Illinois Lt. Gov Juliana Stratton wins Democratic primary for US Senate
video:video.video:description: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton won Tuesday's Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, edging out two sitting members of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: top Iranian security official and Basij force chief killed by Israel
video:video.video:description: Israel said Tuesday it killed two senior Iranian security officials in a major blow aimed at further weakening the Islamic Republic’s leadership.

video:video.video:title: Warsaw Zoo opens 24-hour drop off point for injured birds
video:video.video:description: Warsaw's accident and emergency room for wounded birds treats around 80 cases a day, all found and brought in by locals. (AP Video: Rafal Niedzielski)

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Caracas as Venezuela beats US to win first World Baseball Classic title
video:video.video:description: Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, rebounding from a blown eighth-inning lead to beat the United States 3-2 Tuesday night on Eugenio Suárez’s tiebreaking double in the ninth.

video:video.video:title: World Baseball Classic MVP Maikel Garcia dedicates win to Venezuelans
video:video.video:description: Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, defeating the United States 3–2 on Eugenio Suárez’s tie-breaking double in the ninth inning.

video:video.video:title: French bulldog frenzy may be cooling in US, but dachshunds are riding high
video:video.video:description: Americans still dig French bulldogs, retrievers and German shepherds. But dachshunds are becoming hot dogs.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy arrives in Spain
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Madrid late on Tuesday as part of his European tour.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike hits an apartment building in central Beirut
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike struck an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut, on Wednesday. The Israeli army had warned residents in the building to evacuate about an hour before completely flattening it as day broke

video:video.video:title: It's 'Double Stakes' for Vlad Nikitiuk
video:video.video:description: As Ukrainian crime and corruption drama “Double Stakes” launches in the US and UK, star Vlad Nititiuk looks back on filming the show in Kyiv. (Mar. 18)

video:video.video:title: Former reality TV star Jessie Holmes repeats as champion of the grueling Iditarod sled dog race
video:video.video:description: Holmes guided his dog team across the finish line Tuesday night in the old Gold Rush town of Nome, a Bering Sea coastal community, after traveling for more than nine days.

video:video.video:title: Kew Gardens in London bursts into bloom as spring starts to arrive
video:video.video:description: After a miserable, wet winter, spring has sprung in London, with the city’s Kew Gardens in full bloom. Signs of new life are abundant at this UNESCO World Heritage Site.

video:video.video:title: Metero's sonic boom scares pups in Ohio
video:video.video:description: A suspected meteor that fell over Ohio on Tuesday shook homes and startled residents who heard a boom some compared to an explosion.

video:video.video:title: Strike in Afghanistan by Pakistan kills at least 400, according to Afghan officials
video:video.video:description: Rescue crews were still digging bodies out of the rubble of a drug rehabilitation hospital in the Afghan capital Tuesday morning, after officials there said an overnight Pakistani airstrike killed at least 400 people at the facility.

video:video.video:title: Israel says it killed a top Iranian security official and the Basij force chief
video:video.video:description: Both security official Ali Larijani and Gen. Gholam Reza Soleimani were “eliminated last night," Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: Suspected suicide bombers hit Nigeria’s Maiduguri city, killing 23 and injuring over 100
video:video.video:description: Residents and emergency services earlier told The Associated Press that three explosions were reported in crowded places in Maiduguri, the capital of Borno state, including in a major market and at the entrance of the University of Maiduguri Teaching Hospital.

video:video.video:title: How Poland moved from poor to prosperous in just 13 years
video:video.video:description: Poland is now among the world's 20 largest economies. This is how it happened.

video:video.video:title: Coast Guard crew rescues person stranded on ice in Lake Michigan
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Coast Guard rescued a person stranded on ice near Mackinac Island, Michigan, on Sunday. The crew aboard Coast Guard Cutter Mackinaw was deployed to bring the person aboard the vessel and receive medical care, returning them to stable condition

video:video.video:title: From cart to doorstep in a flash: Amazon says it’s speeding delivery up again
video:video.video:description: Amazon said Tuesday that it has started offering faster U.S. deliveries of selected products for a fee, including pantry staples, clothing, over-the-counter medications, cleaning supplies and electronics.

video:video.video:title: UN chief urges diplomatic push to end war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has asserted once more that “the war in the Middle East must stop."

video:video.video:title: Drone crashes in US Embassy compound in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Two drones were shot down by a defense system at the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad on Tuesday, while a third drone crashed inside the embassy compound, two Iraqi security officials said.

video:video.video:title: UN expresses condolences after airstrike on a hospital in Afghanistan kills hundreds
video:video.video:description: A U.N country representative in Afghanistan on Tuesday expressed its "deepest condolences" to the families of those killed by an airstrike on a drug treatment hospital in Afghanistan.

video:video.video:title: Rubio calls for 'new people in charge’ of Cuba as Trump declares the island 'in very bad shape'
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is calling for "new people in charge" of Cuba as the island experiences its third nationwide blackout in four months.

video:video.video:title: Large crowds brave the cold for New York's St. Patrick's Day Parade
video:video.video:description: Thousands lined New York City streets Tuesday despite the cold weather for the city’s annual St. Patrick’s Day parade, one of the largest celebrations of Irish heritage in the country.

video:video.video:title: Trump says NATO is making a ‘very foolish mistake’ by not assisting US in Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump complains about NATO’s refusal to join the U.S. in the war, calling the decision “disappointing” and bad for the “partnership.”

video:video.video:title: UN expresses concern for fuel shortage in Cuba
video:video.video:description: U.N.'s office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs expressed concern that fuel shortages in Cuba are curbing people's access to essential services across the country, according to U.N.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued from elevated train platform in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: A dog is safe after a rescue from an elevated train platform in Philadelphia. Stranded on a narrow ledge high above around, the dog was saved by ACCT Philly with help from police and transit workers.

video:video.video:title: Mezcal's popularity is booming. That comes with a growing environmental cost in Mexico
video:video.video:description: Production of mezcal in Mexico is booming to meet growing global demand, creating opportunities for producers. It has also come with environmental costs.

video:video.video:title: Trump, Martin and Johnson honor '250 years of Irish American friendship' at annual luncheon
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump, Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin and Speaker of the House Mike Johnson honored the "250 years of Irish American friendship" during the annual Friends of Ireland luncheon on Capitol Hill.

video:video.video:title: Trump team applying pressure to media: Tell the war’s story the way we see it
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and his team are increasing the pressure on journalists to cover the war in the Middle East the way the administration wants.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Not yet 'ready to leave' Iran but predicts an exit in the 'very near future'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he is "not ready to leave" Iran yet, but vowed an exit "in the very near future."

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy calls on UK parliament to help build up anti-drone defences
video:video.video:description: In a speech to members of the British Parliament, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for joint efforts in building up anti-drone defenses amid the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberating in corruption trial of ex-FirstEnergy executives accused of $4.3 million bribe
video:video.video:description: Jurors in Ohio have begun deliberating in the corruption trial of two fired FirstEnergy Corp. executives charged for their alleged roles in the $60 million bribery scheme centered on a $1 billion nuclear plant bailout.

video:video.video:title: Cuba is gradually recovering from an islandwide blackout, but millions are still without electricity
video:video.video:description: Energy is gradually being restored after Monday’s nationwide power outage, but millions remain without electricity. At this stage, Cuba is running on fumes.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 18
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, March 18th: Iran launches new attacks on Israel, Gulf states; Israeli strike kills two senior Iranian officials; Attorney General Pam Bondi subpoenaed over Epstein documents; Illinois Lt.

video:video.video:title: Reported projectile strike at Iran's Bushehr nuclear power complex raises concerns, AP explains
video:video.video:description: In Iran, the Bushehr nuclear power plant complex was hit by a projectile on Tuesday evening but there were no injuries and the plant suffered no damage, the International Atomic Energy Agency said after receiving a report from Tehran.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the resignation of Joe Kent as Trump's counterterrorism chief
video:video.video:description: The U.S. counterterrorism official who resigned Tuesday had been a staunch supporter of President Donald Trump through his 2020 election defeat, the Jan. 6 riots and years of conservative media advocacy and failed congressional bids.

video:video.video:title: Building reduced to rubble after strike that hit central Beirut
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike struck an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut, completely flattening it as day broke on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: How Muslims celebrate the Islamic holiday, Eid al-Fitr
video:video.video:description: The Muslim holy month of Ramadan draws to an end and Muslims around the world will soon start celebrating the holiday of Eid al-Fitr, a joyous time marked with congregational prayers and festivities that include family gatherings and new clothes.

video:video.video:title: Kenya's capital experiments with giving local government workers menstrual leave
video:video.video:description: Nairobi County has introduced Kenya’s first menstrual leave policy for female government staff, aiming to boost health and productivity.

video:video.video:title: The Colosseum has a bright new look after restoration
video:video.video:description: The Colosseum has a bright new look following a restoration using the same travertine marble of ancient Rome to recreate parts of columns from 2,000 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Some say the sharks in Las Vegas eat just as well as the tourists
video:video.video:description: The sharks at Mandalay Bay casino are fed to the same standard as the resort's restaurant. With a diet of wild-caught and sustainable fish, some say the sharks eat just as well as the tourists.

video:video.video:title: Iran war has US farmers worried about the cost and availability of fertilizer
video:video.video:description: Rising fertilizer prices and potential shortages from shipping disruptions due to the Iran war have farmers and U.S. agriculture groups bracing ahead of the spring planting season, raising costs for farmers and potentially pushing some out of business.

video:video.video:title: Vance says there is no risk that Trump would get US into long-term quagmire
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance says there is no risk that President Donald Trump would get the U.S. into a long-term conflict.

video:video.video:title: More than 2 dozen Union Pacific train cars derail in Texas town, no injuries reported
video:video.video:description: More than two dozen cars of a Union Pacific train derailed Wednesday morning near Houston, causing a chemical leak from two of the cars that officials said didn’t pose a threat to the public.

video:video.video:title: Trump angry with NATO allies over Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is expressing anger with NATO’s refusal to join his push to send warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz. (AP Video: Mike Pesoli)

video:video.video:title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, sparking disbelief and an appeal - AP dispatch
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans in Dakar were in disbelief after Senegal was stripped of its Africa Cup of Nations title, two months after the national team returned to the capital to a hero’s welcome following a dramatic win in Morocco.

video:video.video:title: Fed projects one rate cut this year, sees limited economic impact from Iran war
video:video.video:description: Federal Reserve officials left short-term interest rates unchanged Wednesday for the second straight meeting at about 3.6%.

video:video.video:title: 5 popular tax season scams
video:video.video:description: 5 popular tax season scams

video:video.video:title: A look inside Jordan Chiles' final UCLA home meet
video:video.video:description: U.S. Olympic gymnast Jordan Chiles’ last home meet at UCLA was full of team spirit, tears and celebrity appearances.

video:video.video:title: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton wins Democratic primary for US Senate
video:video.video:description: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton wins the Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, edging out two sitting members of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Long lines persist at airports as DHS shutdown affects security
video:video.video:description: Travelers are told to arrive the airport hours ahead of their flights on Wednesday at George Bush Intercontinental Airport in Houston as airline passengers wait in long lines to get through the TSA security screening — lasting hours in some cases (AP video: Lekan Oyekanmi)

video:video.video:title: Regé-Jean Page says being among peers in Hollywood is a ‘privilege’
video:video.video:description: At the Vanity Fair Oscar party, “Bridgerton” star Regé-Jean Page says being surrounded by his Hollywood peers is a privilege.

video:video.video:title: Robert Pattinson on working with Zendaya in multiple films
video:video.video:description: Robert Pattinson suspects Zendaya may have put in a good word for him with “Dune” franchise director Denis Villeneuve.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Caracas after Venezuela wins World Baseball Classic championship
video:video.video:description: For Venezuelans baseball is more than game and the victory against the U.S. in the World Baseball Classic is much more than a championship.

video:video.video:title: Sen. Paul says he'll vote against Mullin for DHS, Sen. Fetterman says he's open-minded
video:video.video:description: Republican Senator Rand Paul and Democratic Senator John Fetterman revealed to reporters whether or not they think Markwayne Mullin should be confirmed as the next homeland security secretary, after a hearing on Capitol Hill on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Illinois Governor Pritzker talks special interest money, AIPAC and election night successes
video:video.video:description: Gov. JB Pritzker on Wednesday blasted special interest money that poured into the state, including from a pro-Israel political group he once supported.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, March 18th: Mullin urges Congress to fund DHS; Iran's natural gas facilities attacked; US stocks fall as Brent crude jumps toward $110 per barrel; Afghanistan and Pakistan call a temporary ceasefire for the Muslim holiday.

video:video.video:title: Rand Paul confronts DHS nominee Mullin in Senate hearing
video:video.video:description: Markwayne Mullin faced questions Wednesday from the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee.

video:video.video:title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he's still 'processing' César Chavez news and urges reflection
video:video.video:description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday said he’s still “processing” the stunning abuse allegations against César Chavez, the long-admired Latino icon, and urged for more reflection.

video:video.video:title: Taliban leaders hold funerals for some of at least 408 killed in airstrike
video:video.video:description: Taliban leaders held funerals in Kabul for those killed in an airstrike earlier in the week. Afghanistan accused Pakistan of killing at least 408 people in an airstrike on the drug rehabilitation hospital in the Afghan capital late Monday.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya wears 2015 Oscars dress to 'The Drama' premiere
video:video.video:description: Zendaya wears the Vivienne Westwood gown she wore to the 2015 Oscars at the premiere of her latest film, “The Drama.”

video:video.video:title: Pulse nightclub demolished nearly 10 years after deadly massacre
video:video.video:description: Crews began demolishing Pulse nightclub in Orlando, Florida, on Wednesday to make way for a memorial that will pay tribute to the 49 people killed in the attack at the LGBTQ-friendly club nearly a decade ago.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes continue to hit Lebanon, including areas in central Beirut and its southern suburbs
video:video.video:description: In renewed fighting with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, Israel has concentrated most strikes where the group has a strong presence in southern Lebanon and Beirut’s southern suburbs.

video:video.video:title: Which countries are allowed to cross the Strait Of Hormuz amid the Iran war?
video:video.video:description: The Strait of Hormuz is a small strip of water connecting the Persian Gulf to the world’s oceans. On a typical day, ships carrying about a fifth of the world’s oil sail out of the Gulf through the narrow passageway, but the war with Iran means it’s effectively closed, hemming in more than 90% of tha

video:video.video:title: How Trump's tariffs have hurt manufacturers instead of helping them
video:video.video:description: There’s a growing body of data showing the tariffs President Donald Trump said would help American factories are, in fact, squashing many of them.

video:video.video:title: Spa day for tortoises: Hungary zoo gives its shell‑ebrity residents the royal treatment
video:video.video:description: Resident tortoises were treated to a comprehensive wellness program by staff at Hungary's Nyíregyháza Zoo.

video:video.video:title: Federal judge ousts DOJ attorney, orders NJ prosecutors to answer under oath
video:video.video:description: Vexed by the Justice Department’s chaotic oversight of federal prosecutions in New Jersey, a judge threw a government attorney out of a hearing and ordered the three officials in charge of the state’s U.S.

video:video.video:title: Half-century after abandoned Florida canal project, plan would restore rivers
video:video.video:description: A remnant of Florida's failed "Panama Canal" from the 1960's -- the Rodman Kirkpatrick Dam-- is blocking manatee and fish migrations and should be removed, environmentalists say.

video:video.video:title: Senegalese soccer fans in disbelief after the country is stripped of Africa Cup title
video:video.video:description: The Confederation of African Football's appeals board on Tuesday ruled Senegal forfeited the final in January by walking off the field and turned its 1-0 win in extra time into a 3-0 default win for Morocco.

video:video.video:title: Iranian media say the women's soccer team has returned to Iran
video:video.video:description: The team returned to the Islamic Republic after several of the players sought asylum in Australia, Iranian media reported on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Gabbard insists only Trump can determine if Iran poses imminent threat - 'false' says Dem. Senator
video:video.video:description: Democratic senators press DNI Tulsi Gabbard about whether she had advised President Donald Trump that Tehran would retaliate against nearby Gulf nations and block the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Veterans react to increasing gas prices, Iran war
video:video.video:description: Bobby Watts, a veteran who served in Operation Desert Storm, said he'd rather see the government spend its tax dollars on helping Americans instead of bombing other countries.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian police operation in Rio favelas leaves at least 8 dead, including local drug boss
video:video.video:description: A police operation in Rio de Janeiro killed seven suspected traffickers, including a gang leader, as well as a resident caught in the crossfire.

video:video.video:title: Kaiser therapists, nurses strike over AI worries in Northern California
video:video.video:description: About 2,400 Kaiser Permanente mental health workers are striking in Northern California over fears the company will replace therapists with artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Pulse nightclub is demolished to make way for a memorial to the 2016 mass shooting
video:video.video:description: Crews began demolishing Pulse nightclub in Orlando, Florida, on Wednesday to make way for a memorial that will pay tribute to the 49 people killed in the attack at the LGBTQ-friendly club nearly a decade ago.

video:video.video:title: Pulse nightclub demolished nearly 10 years after deadly massacre
video:video.video:description: Crews began demolishing Pulse nightclub in Orlando, Florida, on Wednesday to make way for a memorial that will pay tribute to the 49 people killed in the attack at the LGBTQ-friendly club nearly a decade ago.

video:video.video:title: Foreign ministers from Arab and Muslim countries meet in Riyadh over Iranian attacks on Gulf States
video:video.video:description: A meeting of foreign ministers from Arab and Islamic countries took place in Riyadh on Thursday to discuss regional security amid Iranian attacks on Gulf States.

video:video.video:title: Cubans react to tensions between the U.S. and Cuban governments
video:video.video:description: Residents in Cuba on Wednesday weighed on the latest signs of tensions between the U.S. and Cuban governments, hours after President Miguel Díaz-Canel lashed out at recent comments by U.S.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Gosling talks new sci-fi film
video:video.video:description: "Project Hail Mary," based on Andy Weir's novel, is a reminder of what we're capable of as human beings, says its star Ryan Gosling: "That's what human history is, is us doing things that seemed impossible at one point, and then we figure it out."

video:video.video:title: AP staffers turn March Madness into movie brackets
video:video.video:description: AP staffers put a pop culture spin on NCAA March Madness—building movie brackets and debating which film comes out on top.

video:video.video:title: Rhode Island hockey team rallies to win championship after ice rink shooting
video:video.video:description: Coach Chris Librizzi shares how the Blackstone Valley Co-op hockey team returned to the ice after a shooting during one of their matches left three people, family members of one of their players, dead.

video:video.video:title: Building damaged in Tel Aviv after strike
video:video.video:description: A building was damaged in Tel Aviv following a strike early Thursday. Footage captured by The Associated Press showed emergency services at the scene.

video:video.video:title: César Chavez accused of sexually abusing labor rights leader Dolores Huerta, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Labor rights activist Dolores Huerta revealed she was among women and girls who say they were sexually abused by César Chavez, the long-admired Latino icon, while he led the United Farm Workers union.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles residents call for renaming César Chavez street signs following abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: California Rising demands the renaming of streets named after César Chavez due to allegations of sexual abuse during his time as president of the United Farm Workers union.

video:video.video:title: Finns go to sauna to break from outside world and find happiness
video:video.video:description: Leave your phone in the locker, get inside a wood-paneled room with your friends, throw some water on heated rocks and relax - this is the receipt of happiness for Finnish people who have topped the list of the happiest countries in the world for the 9th consecutive time.

video:video.video:title: How to check US airport security lines while TSA agents go unpaid
video:video.video:description: How to check US airport security lines while TSA agents go unpaid

video:video.video:title: AP reporter at scene when bomb squad in northern Israel detonates unexploded ordnance
video:video.video:description: Successive explosions were heard Wednesday afternoon as police detonated an unexploded ordnance in northern Israel.

video:video.video:title: A real possum appears among plush toy animals in Australian airport gift shop
video:video.video:description: On shelves crammed with cuddly toy native animals in an Australian airport gift shop, one fluffy possum stood out: Its big brown eyes were moving.

video:video.video:title: Video shows amur leopard cat stealing fish from otter
video:video.video:description: An Amur leopard cat steals fish from an otter in a video captured at a Russian Nature Reserve.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of strike that hit village near Sidon, Lebanon
video:video.video:description: A strike early Tuesday morning hit a village near the coastal city of Sidon. There were no immediate reports of casualties from the Israeli airstrike, but the Israeli military issued a warning before the strike and said Hezbollah was using the building without giving further details.

video:video.video:title: Eight injured in Russian strike on Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: Eight people were injured Tuesday in a Russian strike in Zaporizhzhia which damaged a terminal of Ukraine's biggest private delivery company, state and regional officials said.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean and authorities won't talk
video:video.video:description: Migrants trying to reach Europe are vanishing in droves in what are known as “invisible shipwrecks” but governments responsible for search and rescue are withholding information about what they know.

video:video.video:title: Interceptions seen in the skies over northern Israel as air raid sirens sound
video:video.video:description: An eyewitness captured video of interceptions in the skies over northern Israel on Tuesday morning, as air raid sirens sounded following a projectile launch from Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, March 17th: Trump says an ex-president said he wishes he had attacked Iran; Explosions heard in Dubai and Qatar; Island-wide blackout in Cuba; Utah woman who wrote book on grief convicted of killing husband.

video:video.video:title: A taste of Ireland — delivered: Mobile pubs roll into New England neighborhoods
video:video.video:description: Two Massachusetts brothers have built a fleet of tiny Irish pubs on wheels — miniature bars they tow to backyards, weddings and neighborhood parties across New England.

video:video.video:title: St Patrick's Day dreams - stay in a converted lighthouse in Ireland
video:video.video:description: It's St. Patrick's day, and visitors to Ireland's rural coastline can find a number of unusual places to stay.

video:video.video:title: Budapest's vintage 'freight trams' celebrate 100 years in transit service
video:video.video:description: The vintage machines were introduced after World War I damaged much of the city's infrastructure. Known locally as mukis, they run on electricity provided by overhead wires and travel on Budapest's vast tram rail network, which is widely regarded as one of the busiest.

video:video.video:title: Fog City sizzles: San Francisco swaps sweaters for sunscreen in rare March heatwave
video:video.video:description: San Francisco is having an unusual March heat wave, with temperatures nearing 90 degrees, marking the city’s hottest start to spring in nearly 20 years.

video:video.video:title: Muskets crack, drums echo as Boston marks 250 years since British evacuation
video:video.video:description: The Evacuation Day commemoration featured Revolutionary War reenactment units, music and a wreath-laying.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, March 17: Top counterterrorism official resigns over war in Iran; Rubio calls for new Cuban leadership; Afghanistan says more than 400 dead in Pakistani airstrike on hospital; crowds brave cold for New York's St.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike kills two Palestinians in Khan Younis
video:video.video:description: At least two Palestinians were killed Tuesday by an Israeli airstrike in the war-torn Gaza Strip, the Palestinian Red Crescent said.

video:video.video:title: More than 400 people killed in strike on Kabul rehabilitation hospital
video:video.video:description: Rescuers recovered more bodies from the rubble of a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul on Tuesday after officials said an overnight airstrike killed more than 400 people in a dramatic escalation of a conflict between Pakistan and Afghanistan that is now in its third week.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued from elevated train platform in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: ACCT Philly, the city’s open-intake shelter, received a call Sunday about a dog stranded high above the ground on a SEPTA train platform.

video:video.video:title: 'Spent my whole day with the Irish': Trump says at annual St. Patrick's Day Shamrock Bowl
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and Ireland Prime Minister Micheál Martin closed out a day of events marking St.

video:video.video:title: Nashville-based reporter for Spanish-language news outlet expected to be freed on bond after ICE detention
video:video.video:description: Estefany Rodríguez Florez' attorneys said that she will likely be able to post bond and be released as soon as tomorrow.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay becomes final South American country to approve Mercosur-European Union trade deal
video:video.video:description: Paraguay became the latest—and final—South American country to ratify the long-awaited trade agreement between Mercosur and the European Union on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Strikes hit Beirut's southern suburbs overnight
video:video.video:description: Two strikes hit Beirut’s southern suburbs early Wednesday, sending flames and smoke into the sky. (AP video by Zakaria Al Khatib)

video:video.video:title: Venezuela rallies past Italy and will face US for World Baseball Classic title
video:video.video:description: Aaron Judge says crowds at this year's World Baseball Classic are "bigger and better" than the World Series. The AP has been there to capture the atmosphere.

video:video.video:title: Two men found with hundreds of ants are charged with illegally dealing in wildlife in Kenya
video:video.video:description: A Chinese national and a Kenyan man were charged Tuesday with unlawfully dealing in wildlife species after they were found in possession of hundreds of live ants stored in specialized tubes.

video:video.video:title: AP reports: Suspected suicide bombers target Nigeria’s Maiduguri city, killing 23 people and injuring over 100
video:video.video:description: Residents and emergency services earlier told The Associated Press that the explosions were reported on Monday night in crowded places in Maiduguri, the capital of Borno state, including at a major market and the entrance of the University of Maiduguri Teaching Hospital.

video:video.video:title: Drone crashes in US Embassy compound in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Two drones were shot down by the defense system at the US Embassy in Baghdad on Tuesday, two Iraqi security officials said. (AP video by Ali Jabar)

video:video.video:title: Republicans push voting bill toward Senate floor
video:video.video:description: Legislation that would require proof of U.S. citizenship for new voters has become a rallying cry for President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Explosions rock Beirut as plumes of smoke fill the sky
video:video.video:description: Large explosions and plumes of smoke were seen in Beirut on Tuesday. Earlier in the day, Lebanon's army said five of its soldiers were hurt after a morning airstrike on the village of Kfar Sir, two of them being seriously wounded.

video:video.video:title: Vice President Vance hosts Ireland's Taoiseach, Micheál Martin, for St. Patrick's Day breakfast
video:video.video:description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and wife, Usha, greeted Ireland's Taoiseach Micheál Martin and wife, Mary O'Shea at the White House Tuesday as part of St.

video:video.video:title: 'Your Rich BFF' gives money tips for couples
video:video.video:description: Finances are one of the most important conversations to have with your significant other, Tu said. Talking about money as a couple can be intimidating, but it’s important for your future.

video:video.video:title: Cuba is gradually recovering from an islandwide blackout, but millions are still without electricity
video:video.video:description: Large parts of Cuba were without power on Tuesday after its third blackout within four months underscored the island's deepening energy and economic crises and rising political tensions with U.S.

video:video.video:title: Gunman killed and employee hospitalized after a shooting at a Georgia VA clinic, police say
video:video.video:description: Police in Georgia say they have responded to a shooting at a Department of Veterans Affairs clinic, where a suspect was shot and killed by police.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya on guarding her private life and 'protecting things that are special'
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of "The Drama," Zendaya spoke about guarding her private life and “protecting things that are special.” (Mar. 17)

video:video.video:title: 'Bridgerton' star Regé-Jean Page says being among peers in Hollywood is a ‘privilege’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Vanity Fair Oscar Party "Bridgerton" star Regé-Jean Page says being among peers in Hollywood is a "privilege," and that he's aware that his audience wants to see him in more rom-coms.

video:video.video:title: At Las Vegas casino's shark tank, the sharks eat just as good as the tourists, dietician says
video:video.video:description: Becky O’Brien always wanted to work with ocean life, but she didn’t realize she’d be able to do the job in the middle of the Las Vegas desert.

video:video.video:title: Mary J. Blige celebrates her journey: 'It's uplifting to have a career like this'
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Vanity Fair Oscar Party, Mary J. Blige reflects on her three-decade career and says she’s still learning new things about herself. (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike hits an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike has struck an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut. The Israeli army had warned residents in the building to evacuate about an hour before completely flattening it as day broke.

video:video.video:title: France’s tallest urban bridge suspended between legs of Paris' Eiffel Tower
video:video.video:description: The temporary attraction allows visitors to walk between the monument’s east and west pillars suspended on a 40-meter-long, 60-meter-high netted bridge accessible from the first floor of Paris’ most famous landmark.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya on 'protecting things that are special'
video:video.video:description: Zendaya’s new ring has led many to believe she’s married to Tom Holland, but she isn't talking about it — and says she's intentional about "protecting things that are special."

video:video.video:title: State Department cut jobs with deep expertise in Middle East as Iran crisis escalates
video:video.video:description: The widening war tied to Iran is exposing big gaps at the State Department, especially in the bureau that handles the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Ryan Gosling leads 'Project Hail Mary' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Ryan Gosling didn't set off on his latest space odyssey in just a boring astronaut suit. The 45-year-old star of "Project Hail Mary" credits his kids — and inspiration from Val Kilmer — for his stylish astro-nerd look in the sci-fi film.

video:video.video:title: Saudi Arabia’s foreign minister harshly criticizes Iran after overnight attacks
video:video.video:description: Saudi Arabia's foreign minister said the strikes against the kingdom and neighboring states underscored growing regional risks, and called for an end to attacks on countries not directly involved in the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Behind the scenes of New York's top pleating house
video:video.video:description: Artisan George Kalajian, who crafted the pleats on Lady Gaga’s Super Bowl gown in hours, works a skill that dates before ancient Greece.

video:video.video:title: Greeks and their pets arrive in Athens on a repatriation flight from Abu Dhabi
video:video.video:description: Emotional scenes unfolded at Athens airport as small dogs leaped for joy after being let out of their special travel carry cases.

video:video.video:title: Former Phoenix city councilor who worked with Chavez reacts to sex abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: Former Phoenix City Council member Mary Rose Wilcox, who marched alongside César Chavez at protests in the 1970s, said she is dismayed at the sex abuse allegations against the long-admired Latino icon.

video:video.video:title: Iraqis prepare for Eid al Fitr amid economic crisis and Iran war
video:video.video:description: Iraqis crowded in a Baghdad market on Wednesday as they shopped and prepared for the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Fitr amid an economic crisis triggered by the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands in Tbilisi mourn Georgia’s late Patriarch Ilia II
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people took part in a procession in Tbilisi on Wednesday, accompanying the coffin of Georgia’s Patriarch to the Holy Trinity Cathedral.

video:video.video:title: Europe provides aid to hospitals in Cuba as the island struggles with oil embargo and U.S. pressure
video:video.video:description: The 5 tons of medical supplies donated by European organizations began being delivered to hospitals in Havana on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: In Lebanon, displacement and hardship marring run-up to Eid al-Fitr holiday for many
video:video.video:description: As the holy month of Ramadan draws to an end and Muslims worldwide prepare for the typically joyous holiday of Eid al-Fitr, Lebanon has crossed a grim milestone.
